steps.title: Estimate the intercept
steps.content: The data trend downward and appear to be near $90$ when $x$ is near $0$. So the model should have a negative slope and a $y$-intercept near $90$.

steps.title: Estimate the slope
steps.content: The data drop by about $90$ units over about $9$ horizontal units, so the slope should be close to $$ \frac{-90}{9}=-10 $$ The equation with intercept $90$ and slope $-10$ is $y=90-10x$. The answer is D.
